2604.26051.title: Evaluating the Alignment Between GeoAI Explanations and Domain Knowledge in Satellite-Based Flood Mapping
2604.26051.paper_summary: The increasing number of satellites has improved the temporal resolution of Earth observation, making satellite-based flood mapping a promising approach for operational flood monitoring. Deep learning-based approaches for flood mapping using satellite imagery, an important application within Geospatial Artificial Intelligence (GeoAI), have shown improved predictive performance by learning complex spatial and spectral patterns from large volumes of remote sensing data. However, the opaque decision-making processes of deep learning models remain a major barrier to their integration into critical scientific and operational workflows. This highlights the need for a systematic assessment of whether model explanations align with established domain knowledge in remote sensing. To address this research gap, this study introduces the ADAGE (Alignment between Domain Knowledge And GeoAI Explanation Evaluation) framework. The proposed framework is designed to systematically evaluate how well explanations of deep learning models align with established remote sensing knowledge, particularly regarding the distinctive spectral properties of the Earth's surface. The ADAGE framework employs Channel-Group SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) method to estimate the contributions of grouped input channels to pixel-level predictions. Experiments on two satellite-based flood mapping tasks demonstrate that the ADAGE framework can (1) quantitatively assess the alignment between model explanations and reference explanations derived from domain knowledge and (2) help domain experts identify misaligned explanations through alignment scores. This study contributes to bridging the gap between explainability and domain knowledge in GeoAI for Earth observation, enhancing the applicability of GeoAI models in scientific and operational workflows.
2604.26051.paper_summary_zh: 增加的衛星數量改善了地球觀測的時間解析度，使基於衛星的洪水映射成為運營洪水監測的一種有前景的方法。基於深度學習的洪水映射方法利用衛星影像，這是地理空間人工智慧（GeoAI）中的一個重要應用，通過從大量遙感數據中學習複雜的空間和光譜模式，顯示出改進的預測性能。然而，深度學習模型的不透明決策過程仍然是將其整合到關鍵科學和運營工作流程中的主要障礙。這突顯了系統評估模型解釋是否與遙感中既定領域知識一致的必要性。為了解決這一研究空白，本研究介紹了ADAGE（領域知識與GeoAI解釋評估之間的對齊）框架。所提出的框架旨在系統性地評估深度學習模型的解釋與既定遙感知識之間的對齊程度，特別是關於地球表面的獨特光譜特性。ADAGE框架採用了Channel-Group SHAP（SHapley Additive exPlanations）方法來估計分組輸入通道對像素級預測的貢獻。對兩個基於衛星的洪水映射任務的實驗表明，ADAGE框架可以（1）定量評估模型解釋與來自領域知識的參考解釋之間的對齊程度，以及（2）通過對齊分數幫助領域專家識別不一致的解釋。本研究有助於縮小GeoAI在地球觀測中解釋性與領域知識之間的差距，增強GeoAI模型在科學和運營工作流程中的適用性。
2604.26051.author: Hyunho Lee et.al.
